    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_height)
    .polyline(full_profile)
    .close()
    .extrude(-pocket_depth)
)

result = result.cut(logo_cut)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
L = 40.0            # Overall Length (X)
W = 40.0            # Overall Width (Y)
H = 40.0            # Overall Height (Z)

t_back = 10.0       # Back wall thickness
t_right = 10.0      # Right arm thickness
t_left_top = 10.0   # Left arm top thickness
t_left_bot = 18.0   # Left arm bottom thickness
h_ledge = 20.0      # Height of the ledge/step on the left inner arm

# 1. Base solid block
body = cq.Workplane("XY").box(L, W, H, centered=(False, False, False))

# 2. Main U-channel cutout - Top section (thinner left wall)
cut_top = cq.Workplane("XY").box(
    L - t_back, W - t_right - t_left_top, H - h_ledge, centered=(False, False, False)
).translate((t_back, t_left_top, h_ledge))
         
# 3. Main U-channel cutout - Bottom section (thicker left wall)
cut_bot = cq.Workplane("XY").box(
    L - t_back, W - t_right - t_left_bot, h_ledge, centered=(False, False, False)
).translate((t_back, t_left_bot, 0))

body = body.cut(cut_top).cut(cut_bot)

# 4. Vertical notch on the inner face of the left arm (top part)
n_w = 4.0           # Notch width (along X)
n_d = 5.0           # Notch depth (along Y)
n_x = 22.0          # Notch position (X coordinate)

notch = cq.Workplane("XY").box(
    n_w, n_d, H - h_ledge, centered=(False, False, False)
).translate((n_x, t_left_top - n_d, h_ledge))
          
body = body.cut(notch)

# 5. Cylindrical Pin on outer left face
pin_x = 32.0        # Pin position X
pin_z = 10.0        # Pin position Z
pin_r = 5.0         # Pin radius
pin_l = 15.0        # Pin extrusion length

# Extrude from the XZ plane (which is the Y=0 outer face) in the -Y direction
pin = cq.Workplane("XZ").center(pin_x, pin_z).circle(pin_r).extrude(-pin_l)
result = body.union(pin)

# 6. Fillets & Detailing
# Dome/round the tip of the cylindrical pin
result = result.edges("<Y").fillet(4.5)

# Round the outer vertical edges to match the aesthetic
try:
    result = result.edges("|Z").edges(">X").fillet(1.5)  # Front corners of the U-arms
    result = result.edges("|Z").edges(">Y").fillet(1.5)  # Right outer corners
    result = result.edges("|Z").edges("<X").fillet(1.5)  # Back outer corners
except:
    pass

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
thickness = 5.0
head_radius = 40.0

# 1. Base Head
head = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(head_radius).extrude(thickness)

# 2. Spikes (Hair/Horns)
spikes_pts = [
    (-32, 24),
    (-35, 75),  # Left spike tip
    (-15, 38),
    (-5, 80),   # Middle spike tip
    (8, 38),
    (18, 60),   # Right spike tip
    (28, 28),
    (0, 0)      # Anchor inside the head
]
spikes = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(spikes_pts).close().extrude(thickness)
body = head.union(spikes)

# 3. Facial Features Cutouts
# Eyes
left_eye = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-14, 10, 0), rotate=(0, 0, -15))
    .ellipse(7.5, 14.0)
    .extrude(thickness)
)
right_eye = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(14, 10, 0), rotate=(0, 0, 5))
    .ellipse(7.0, 13.5)
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Nose and Cheeks
nose = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, -2).circle(3.5).extrude(thickness)
left_cheek = cq.Workplane("XY").center(-33, 3).circle(2.5).extrude(thickness)
right_cheek = cq.Workplane("XY").center(33, 3).circle(2.5).extrude(thickness)

# Mouth with teeth
mouth = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .moveTo(-28, -12)
    # Upper lip curving to teeth
    .threePointArc((-18, -17), (-11, -19))
    .lineTo(-4, -28)   # Left tooth tip
    .lineTo(0, -20)    # Center between teeth
    .lineTo(6, -28)    # Right tooth tip
    .lineTo(12, -19)   # End of right tooth
    .threePointArc((20, -17), (28, -12))
    # Lower lip curve
    .threePointArc((0, -36), (-28, -12))
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# 4. Final Boolean Operations
result = (
    body
    .cut(left_eye)
    .cut(right_eye)
    .cut(nose)
    .cut(left_cheek)
    .cut(right_cheek)
    .cut(mouth)
)

result = cq.Workplane("XY").box(100, 100, 80).edges("|Z").fillet(4)

# 2. Main top pocket

result = result.faces(">Z").workplane().placeSketch(sk_pocket).cutBlind(-20)

# 3. Lowered shelf on the back-left rim
# Centered to cut through the X- rim (from X=-55 to -30)
sk_shelf = cq.Sketch().rect(25, 40).vertices().fillet(3)
result = result.faces(">Z").workplane().center(-42.5, 0).placeSketch(sk_shelf).cutBlind(-10)

# 4. Semi-circular notches on opposite rims
# Notch on the right (X+) rim
result = result.faces(">Z").workplane().center(30, 0).circle(8).cutBlind(-20)
# Notch on the left (X-) shelf
result = result.faces(">Z").workplane().center(-30, 0).circle(8).cutBlind(-20)

# 5. Side L-shaped cutout on the front-right (X+) face
# Create a workplane on the X+ face and draw the cut profile
wp_side = cq.Workplane("YZ").workplane(offset=50)
pts = [
    (-30, -45),
    (-30, 0),
    (-10, 0),
    (-10, 20),
    (55, 20),
    (55, -20),
    (-10, -20),
    (-10, -45)
]
sk_side = cq.Sketch().polygon(pts).vertices().fillet(4)
result = result.cut(wp_side.placeSketch(sk_side).extrude(-15))

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the geometric model
L = 160.0             # Overall length of the main bracket
H = 30.0              # Height of the vertical front face
T = 3.0               # Thickness of the material
D = 22.0              # Depth of the bottom horizontal flange

hole_radius = 2.0     # Radius of the front mounting holes
hole_offset_x = 8.0   # Horizontal distance from edge to hole center
hole_offset_z = 24.0  # Vertical distance from bottom to hole center

tab_L = 15.0          # Length of the backward extending tabs
tab_W = 4.0           # Width of each tab
tab_spacing = 16.0    # Center-to-center distance between the two tabs
tab_cx = L / 2        # X-center of the tabs pair

cut_L = 35.0          # Length of the slot cutout on the bottom flange
cut_W = 10.0          # Width of the slot cutout
cut_cx = L - 15.0 - cut_L/2  # X-center of the slot cutout
cut_cy = T + (D - T)/2       # Y-center of the slot cutout

pin_R = 1.5           # Radius of the right-side pivot pin
pin_L = 5.0           # Length of the pivot pin sticking out
pin_Z = 6.0           # Z-height of the pivot pin

# 1. Base L-shape Profile
# We draw the profile in the YZ plane and extrude it along the X axis
profile = cq.Workplane("YZ").polyline([
    (0, H),
    (0, 0),
    (D, 0),
    (D, T),
    (T, T),
    (T, H)
]).close()

result = profile.extrude(L)

# 2. Holes in the front face
# The front face is pointing in the -Y direction.
result = result.faces("<Y").workplane(centerOption="CenterOfBoundBox") \
               .pushPoints([
                   (-L/2 + hole_offset_x, hole_offset_z - H/2), 
                   (L/2 - hole_offset_x, hole_offset_z - H/2)
               ]) \
               .hole(hole_radius * 2)

# 3. Two tabs on the back edge of the bottom flange
# Left tab rectangular body and semi-circular end
tab1 = cq.Workplane("XY").center(tab_cx - tab_spacing/2, D).rect(tab_W, tab_L, centered=(True, False)).extrude(T)
tab1_end = cq.Workplane("XY").center(tab_cx - tab_spacing/2, D + tab_L).circle(tab_W/2).extrude(T)

# Right tab rectangular body and semi-circular end
tab2 = cq.Workplane("XY").center(tab_cx + tab_spacing/2, D).rect(tab_W, tab_L, centered=(True, False)).extrude(T)
tab2_end = cq.Workplane("XY").center(tab_cx + tab_spacing/2, D + tab_L).circle(tab_W/2).extrude(T)

# Combine the tabs into the main body
result = result.union(tab1).union(tab1_end).union(tab2).union(tab2_end)

# 4. Slot Cutout on the right side of the bottom flange
# Creating a cutting tool shaped like a stadium (slot)
cutter = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(cut_cx, cut_cy, -T)) \
           .slot2D(cut_L - cut_W, cut_W, 0) \
           .extrude(T * 3)

result = result.cut(cutter)

# 5. Right Pivot Pin
# Adds a small cylinder sticking out to the right (+X direction)
pin_right = cq.Workplane("YZ", origin=(L, T/2, pin_Z)).circle(pin_R).extrude(pin_L)
result = result.union(pin_right)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions
thickness = 6.0
fillet_radius = 2.9
hub_radius = 34.0
orbit_radius = 45.0
ring_outer_radius = 15.0
ring_inner_radius = 9.0
center_hole_radius = 12.0
inner_hole_radius = 4.0
inner_orbit_radius = 22.0

# Generate coordinates for circular patterns
pts_outer = [
    (orbit_radius * math.cos(math.radians(i * 45)), orbit_radius * math.sin(math.radians(i * 45)))
    for i in range(8)
]

pts_inner = [
    (inner_orbit_radius * math.cos(math.radians(i * 45 + 22.5)), inner_orbit_radius * math.sin(math.radians(i * 45 + 22.5)))
    for i in range(8)
]

# Build main body
base_hub = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(hub_radius).extrude(thickness)
outer_rings = cq.Workplane("XY").pushPoints(pts_outer).circle(ring_outer_radius).extrude(thickness)

result = base_hub.union(outer_rings)

# Create cutting tools
tool_center = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(center_hole_radius).extrude(thickness)
tool_outer_holes = cq.Workplane("XY").pushPoints(pts_outer).circle(ring_inner_radius).extrude(thickness)
tool_inner_holes = cq.Workplane("XY").pushPoints(pts_inner).circle(inner_hole_radius).extrude(thickness)

# Perform cuts
result = result.cut(tool_center).cut(tool_outer_holes).cut(tool_inner_holes)

# Apply global fillets to all horizontal (top and bottom) edges
result = result.edges("not |Z").fillet(fillet_radius)

# Parameters

    # Bottom-left angled shape
    [(-30, -20), (-10, -10), (0, -20), (-20, -35)],
    
    # Central dominant structure
    [(-5, 35), (25, 35), (5, 5), (-5, 5)],

]

import cadquery as cq

# Dimensions
total_width = 60
base_length = 55
base_thickness = 10
prong_width = 20
prong_radius = 10
u_cut_radius = 10
u_cut_y = 35

skirt_height = 5
front_wall_depth = 10

post_width = 10
post_height = 15
post_depth = 10

# Base Plate Body
base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(total_width, base_length, base_thickness, centered=(True, False, False))

# Add rounded prong tips
left_tip = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=base_thickness/2).center(-total_width/2 + prong_radius, base_length).cylinder(base_thickness, prong_radius)
right_tip = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=base_thickness/2).center(total_width/2 - prong_radius, base_length).cylinder(base_thickness, prong_radius)
base = base.union(left_tip).union(right_tip)

# Cut out the U-shape
u_cut_cyl = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, u_cut_y).cylinder(base_thickness * 3, u_cut_radius)
u_cut_rect = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, u_cut_y).rect(u_cut_radius * 2, base_length, centered=(True, False)).extrude(base_thickness * 2, both=True)
base = base.cut(u_cut_cyl).cut(u_cut_rect)

# Add downward front skirt
skirt = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-skirt_height).box(total_width, front_wall_depth, skirt_height, centered=(True, False, False))
base = base.union(skirt)

# Add upward posts at the front corners
left_post = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=base_thickness).center(-total_width/2 + post_width/2, 0).box(post_width, post_depth, post_height, centered=(True, False, False))
right_post = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=base_thickness).center(total_width/2 - post_width/2, 0).box(post_width, post_depth, post_height, centered=(True, False, False))
base = base.union(left_post).union(right_post)

# Fillet the top left and right edges of the posts
base = base.faces(">Z").edges("||Y").fillet(2)

# Holes in the prongs
prong_hole_centers = [(-total_width/2 + prong_radius, base_length), (total_width/2 - prong_radius, base_length)]
prong_holes = cq.Workplane("XY").pushPoints(prong_hole_centers).circle(3.5).extrude(50, both=True)
base = base.cut(prong_holes)

# Small mounting holes on the main plate
small_hole_centers = [(0, 18), (-15, 18), (15, 18)]
small_holes = cq.Workplane("XY").pushPoints(small_hole_centers).circle(2.0).extrude(50, both=True)
base = base.cut(small_holes)

# Horizontal holes through the posts
post_hole_z = base_thickness + post_height / 2
post_hole_centers = [(-total_width/2 + post_width/2, post_hole_z), (total_width/2 - post_width/2, post_hole_z)]
post_holes = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=-20).pushPoints(post_hole_centers).circle(2.5).extrude(50, both=True)
base = base.cut(post_holes)

result = base

# Parameters for the parametric model

cut_W = 12.0      # Depth of the corner cutouts
D_hole = 12.0     # Diameter of the central hole
R_vert = 3.0      # Fillet radius for outer vertical edges
R_horiz = 1.5     # Fillet radius for all horizontal and hole edges

# Define the 2D profile points centered at the origin
# The flat side is at y = -W/2, and the stepped side is at y = W/2
pts = [
    (-L/2, -W/2),
    (L/2, -W/2),
    (L/2, W/2 - cut_W),
    (L/2 - cut_L, W/2 - cut_W),
    (L/2 - cut_L, W/2),
    (-L/2 + cut_L, W/2),
    (-L/2 + cut_L, W/2 - cut_W),
    (-L/2, W/2 - cut_W)
]

# Build the 3D geometry
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polyline(pts)
    .close()
    .extrude(T)
    # Fillet the vertical edges (corners)
    .edges("|Z")
    .fillet(R_vert)
    # Create the central through-hole
    .faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .hole(D_hole)
    # Apply a global fillet to round the top/bottom faces and hole edges
    .edges()
    .fillet(R_horiz)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric Dimensions
bed_width = 160.0
bed_length = 200.0
base_h = 25.0
headboard_h = 80.0
headboard_t = 12.0

mattress_w = 146.0
mattress_l = 180.0
mattress_h = 30.0
mattress_fillet = 6.0

pillow_w = 55.0
pillow_l = 35.0
pillow_h = 10.0
pillow_fillet = 3.5
pillow_gap = 10.0

# 1. Create Base Frame (L-shaped profile extruded symmetrically)
profile_pts = [
    (-bed_length/2.0, 0),
    (bed_length/2.0, 0),
    (bed_length/2.0, base_h),
    (-bed_length/2.0 + headboard_t, base_h),
    (-bed_length/2.0 + headboard_t, headboard_h),
    (-bed_length/2.0, headboard_h)
]

base_frame = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .polyline(profile_pts)
    .close()
    .extrude(bed_width/2.0, both=True)
)

# 2. Create Mattress
mat_y_center = -bed_length/2.0 + headboard_t + mattress_l/2.0 + 3.0

mattress = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_h)
    .center(0, mat_y_center)
    .box(mattress_w, mattress_l, mattress_h, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges().fillet(mattress_fillet)
)

# 3. Create Pillows
pillow_y_center = -bed_length/2.0 + headboard_t + pillow_l/2.0 + 6.0
pillow_x_offset = pillow_w/2.0 + pillow_gap/2.0

pillow1 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_h + mattress_h)
    .center(pillow_x_offset, pillow_y_center)
    .box(pillow_w, pillow_l, pillow_h, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges().fillet(pillow_fillet)
)

pillow2 = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=base_h + mattress_h)
    .center(-pillow_x_offset, pillow_y_center)
    .box(pillow_w, pillow_l, pillow_h, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges().fillet(pillow_fillet)
)

# Assemble Final Geometry
result = base_frame.union(mattress).union(pillow1).union(pillow2)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
height = 45.0
outer_radius = 16.0
fillet_radius = 2.5

inner_radius = 6.5
spline_depth = 3.0
spline_width = 1.6
num_splines = 16

# 1. Create the main cylindrical body
body = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(outer_radius).extrude(height)

# 2. Fillet the outer top and bottom edges
# Before any cuts, the only circular edges are the top and bottom outer boundaries
body = body.edges("%CIRCLE").fillet(fillet_radius)

# 3. Cut the central internal bore hole
body = body.faces(">Z").workplane().circle(inner_radius).cutThruAll()

# 4. Cut the internal splines (keyways)
# We use an overlap to ensure the rectangular cuts fully open into the central bore
overlap = 2.0
rect_length = spline_depth + overlap
# Position the center of the rectangle so its inner edge is well inside the central hole
rect_center_r = inner_radius + (spline_depth / 2.0) - (overlap / 2.0)

result = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane()
    # Create a polar array for the splines. 
    # Local X-axis of each item points radially outward.
    .polarArray(rect_center_r, 0, 360, num_splines)
    # rect length is radial (X), width is tangential (Y)
    .rect(rect_length, spline_width)
    .cutThruAll()
)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters
bar_len = 250
bar_w = 15
base_th = 10
truss_len = 140
truss_w = 40
bracket_th = 15
bracket_h = 100
base_z = 15
hole_radius = 2.5

# Main assembly
# Back bar
back_bar = cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(0, bar_w/2, base_z + base_th/2)).box(bar_len, bar_w, base_th)

# Truss plate (horizontal base)
truss_plate = cq.Workplane("XY").transformed(offset=(0, bar_w + truss_w/2, base_z + base_th/2)).box(truss_len, truss_w, base_th)
base = back_bar.union(truss_plate)

# Cutouts in truss plate
def make_triangle(v1, v2, v3):
    return cq.Workplane("XY").polyline([v1, v2, v3]).close().extrude(100)

t1 = make_triangle((-55, bar_w + 10), (-25, bar_w + 10), (-40, bar_w + truss_w - 10))
t2 = make_triangle((-15, bar_w + truss_w - 10), (15, bar_w + truss_w - 10), (0, bar_w + 10))
t3 = make_triangle((25, bar_w + 10), (55, bar_w + 10), (40, bar_w + truss_w - 10))

base = base.cut(t1).cut(t2).cut(t3)

# Vertical holes in the base (extensions and truss web)
base = base.faces(">Z").workplane().pushPoints([
    (-105, bar_w/2), (105, bar_w/2),  # Holes on back bar extensions
    (-20, bar_w + 20), (20, bar_w + 20)  # Holes on solid truss diagonals
]).hole(hole_radius * 2)

# Brackets
bracket_pts = [(0, 0), (0, bracket_h), (20, bracket_h), (bar_w + truss_w, 30), (bar_w + truss_w, 0)]
bracket_profile = cq.Workplane("YZ").polyline(bracket_pts).close()

# Inside cutout for bracket
cutout = cq.Workplane("YZ").polyline([(15, 15), (15, 75), (42, 15)]).close()
bracket_2d = bracket_profile.cut(cutout)

# Extrude brackets
left_bracket = bracket_2d.extrude(bracket_th).translate((-truss_len/2 - bracket_th, 0, 0))
right_bracket = bracket_2d.extrude(bracket_th).translate((truss_len/2, 0, 0))

# Combine everything
result = base.union(left_bracket).union(right_bracket)

# Add horizontal and slanted holes manually for robustness
cyls = None

# Helper to add subtractive cylinders
def add_hole(x, y, z, dx, dy, dz, length=30):
    global cyls
    c = cq.Solid.makeCylinder(hole_radius, length, cq.Vector(x, y, z), cq.Vector(dx, dy, dz))
    if cyls is None:
        cyls = cq.Workplane(c)
    else:
        cyls = cyls.add(c)

bracket_centers_x = [-truss_len/2 - bracket_th/2, truss_len/2 + bracket_th/2]

for bx in bracket_centers_x:
    # Front face holes
    add_hole(bx, 60, 15, 0, -1, 0)
    
    # Back face holes (visible in the cutout)
    add_hole(bx, -10, 40, 0, 1, 0)
    add_hole(bx, -10, 60, 0, 1, 0)
    
    # Slanted face holes
    # Slanted edge goes from (20, 100) to (55, 30). Normal is roughly (0, 2, 1), going inward is (0, -2, -1)
    pts_slanted = [
        (28.75, 82.5),
        (37.50, 65.0),
        (46.25, 47.5)
    ]
    for sy, sz in pts_slanted:
        add_hole(bx, sy + 5, sz + 2.5, 0, -2, -1)

# Cut all horizontal and slanted holes
if cyls is not None:
    result = result.cut(cyls)

import cadquery as cq

class SharpInnerEdges(cq.Selector):
    def filter(self, objectList):
        res = []
        for o in objectList:
            if o.geomType() == 'LINE':
                # Check if the edge is vertical
                v = o.tangentAt(0.5)
                if abs(v.z) > 0.99:
                    c = o.Center()
                    r = (c.x**2 + c.y**2)**0.5
                    # Select only the intersection edges which are around R=42
                    if 38 < r < 44:
                        res.append(o)
        return res

# 1. Create the main disk with the central basic hole
base = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(80).circle(42).extrude(2)

# 2. Create the 5 inward pointing lobes
lobes = cq.Workplane("XY").polarArray(46, 0, 360, 5).circle(18).extrude(2)

# Union the lobes with the base to form the wavy inner cutout
result = base.union(lobes)

# 3. Apply 3D fillets to the sharp intersection edges to smooth the wavy profile
result = result.edges(SharpInnerEdges()).fillet(3.5)

# 4. Add the 10 angled slots around the face
for i in range(10):
    result = (
        result.faces(">Z").workplane()
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, i * 36))
        .center(66, 0)
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, 45))
        .slot2D(20, 3)
        .cutBlind(-5)
    )

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
length = 140.0
handle_width = 16.0
thickness = 8.0
head_r = 18.0
cut_r = 8.0
opening = 16.0
transition_r = 10.0
edge_fillet = 1.5

# 1. Create a 2D sketch of the wrench base and fillet the inner corners
s = (
    cq.Sketch()
    .rect(length, handle_width)
    .push([(-length / 2.0, 0), (length / 2.0, 0)])
    .circle(head_r)
    .clean()
    .reset()
    .vertices()
    .fillet(transition_r)
)

# 2. Extrude the base symmetrically
base = cq.Workplane("XY").placeSketch(s).extrude(thickness / 2.0, both=True)

# 3. Create the cut tools for the open jaws
left_cyl = cq.Workplane("XY").center(-length / 2.0, 0).cylinder(thickness + 2.0, cut_r)
left_box = cq.Workplane("XY").center(-length / 2.0 - head_r / 2.0, 0).box(head_r, opening, thickness + 2.0)

right_cyl = cq.Workplane("XY").center(length / 2.0, 0).cylinder(thickness + 2.0, cut_r)
right_box = cq.Workplane("XY").center(length / 2.0 + head_r / 2.0, 0).box(head_r, opening, thickness + 2.0)

# Combine cut tools
cuts = left_cyl.union(left_box).union(right_cyl).union(right_box)

# 4. Perform the boolean cut
wrench = base.cut(cuts)

# 5. Apply fillets to the top and bottom edges for a smooth, finished look
result = wrench.edges(">Z or <Z").fillet(edge_fillet)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions for a standard 2020 T-slot aluminum extrusion
W = 20.0              # Profile width and height
L = 30.0              # Extrusion length
center_hole_d = 5.0   # Center hole diameter
corner_r = 1.5        # Outer corner fillet radius

# Slot parametric dimensions
neck_w = 6.2          # Width of the outer slot opening
neck_d = 1.8          # Depth of the straight neck section
inner_w = 11.5        # Maximum inner width of the T-slot
inner_d = 6.0         # Total depth of the slot from the outer face

# Derived coordinates
half_w = W / 2.0
half_neck = neck_w / 2.0
half_inner = inner_w / 2.0
angle_drop = half_inner - half_neck

# Create the base block and apply corner fillets
base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(W, W, L)
base = base.edges("|Z").fillet(corner_r)

# Cut the central hole
base = base.faces(">Z").workplane().circle(center_hole_d / 2.0).extrude(-L, combine="cut")

# Define the 2D profile for the slot cutter
# Extending the top slightly past the edge (+1.0) guarantees a clean cut
slot_pts = [
    (half_neck, half_w + 1.0),
    (half_neck, half_w - neck_d),
    (half_inner, half_w - neck_d - angle_drop),
    (half_inner, half_w - inner_d),
    (-half_inner, half_w - inner_d),
    (-half_inner, half_w - neck_d - angle_drop),
    (-half_neck, half_w - neck_d),
    (-half_neck, half_w + 1.0)
]

# Create the cutter solid. Offset and extruded longer to ensure complete intersection
cutter = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-L).polyline(slot_pts).close().extrude(L * 2)

# Subtract the cutter from the base on all 4 sides
result = base
for i in range(4):
    rotated_cutter = cutter.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), 90 * i)
    result = result.cut(rotated_cutter)

# Extrusion parameters
width = 20.0

corner_radius = 1.5

slot_depth_straight = 1.8
slot_width_inner = 12.0
slot_depth_inner = 6.0
slot_angle_depth = 4.0

p1_y = width / 2 + 1.0  # Extend slightly outside to ensure clean cut

p3_y = width / 2 - slot_angle_depth

slot_pts = [
    (p1_x, p1_y),

    (p3_x, p3_y),
    (p4_x, p4_y),
    (-p4_x, p4_y),

]

import math

# Parametric dimensions

num_points = 360

# Generate points for the continuous gear/sprocket profile
pts = []

    theta = math.radians(i * 360.0 / num_points)
    # Sinusoidal variation for smooth rounded teeth and valleys
    r = r_avg + amp * math.cos(num_teeth * theta)

    cq.Workplane("XY")

    .circle(hole_radius) # Add central hole
    .extrude(thickness)
)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions
R = 100.0             # Centerline radius
W = 28.0              # Width of the plate
T = 4.0               # Thickness of the plate
angle_span = 80.0     # Total angle of the curved plate
half_angle = angle_span / 2.0

# Create the main curved base plate using revolve
profile = cq.Workplane("XZ").rect(W, T).translate((R, 0))
plate = profile.revolve(angle_span, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
plate = plate.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), -half_angle)

# Function to add rounded end caps
def add_end_cap(obj, angle):
    x = R * math.cos(math.radians(angle))
    y = R * math.sin(math.radians(angle))
    cap = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(x, y, 0)).cylinder(T, W / 2.0)
    return obj.union(cap)

plate = add_end_cap(plate, half_angle)
plate = add_end_cap(plate, -half_angle)

result = plate

# Cut 5 large circular holes
r_hole = 8.0
hole_angles = [-24, -12, 0, 12, 24]
for a in hole_angles:
    x = R * math.cos(math.radians(a))
    y = R * math.sin(math.radians(a))
    cutter = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(x, y, 0)).cylinder(T * 3, r_hole)
    result = result.cut(cutter)

# Function to cut curved slots at the ends
w_slot = 10.0
def cut_curved_slot(obj, R_center, w, a_start, a_end, thickness):
    span = a_end - a_start
    prof = cq.Workplane("XZ").rect(w, thickness * 3).translate((R_center, 0))
    slot = prof.revolve(span, (0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1))
    slot = slot.rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 0, 1), a_start)
    
    x1 = R_center * math.cos(math.radians(a_start))
    y1 = R_center * math.sin(math.radians(a_start))
    e1 = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(x1, y1, 0)).cylinder(thickness * 3, w / 2.0)
    
    x2 = R_center * math.cos(math.radians(a_end))
    y2 = R_center * math.sin(math.radians(a_end))
    e2 = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(x2, y2, 0)).cylinder(thickness * 3, w / 2.0)
    
    return obj.cut(slot.union(e1).union(e2))

result = cut_curved_slot(result, R, w_slot, 32, 38, T)
result = cut_curved_slot(result, R, w_slot, -38, -32, T)

# Cut alternating small holes along inner and outer edges
r_small = 1.5
edge_margin = 3.5
small_hole_data = [
    (30, R - W / 2.0 + edge_margin),   # inner
    (18, R + W / 2.0 - edge_margin),   # outer
    (6,  R - W / 2.0 + edge_margin),   # inner
    (-6, R + W / 2.0 - edge_margin),   # outer
    (-18, R - W / 2.0 + edge_margin),  # inner
    (-30, R + W / 2.0 - edge_margin),  # outer
]

for a, r_pos in small_hole_data:
    x = r_pos * math.cos(math.radians(a))
    y = r_pos * math.sin(math.radians(a))
    cutter = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(x, y, 0)).cylinder(T * 3, r_small)
    result = result.cut(cutter)

# Apply a fillet to soften all edges
try:
    result = result.edges().fillet(0.8)
except:
    pass

]

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
L = 140.0
H = 40.0
W_arm = 16.0
H_base = 18.0
thickness = 10.0
pocket_depth = 6.0

wt_side = 4.0
wt_bottom = 7.0
wt_top = 4.0
wt_inner = 4.0

r_out = 5.0
r_in = 4.0
r_pocket = 3.5

tab_w = 12.0
tab_l = 30.0
tab_t = 3.0
tab_x = [-45, -18, 18, 45]
tab_fillet = 2.5
tab_front_fillet = 1.0

# 1. Main Outer Body Profile
body = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .moveTo(0, 0)
    .lineTo(L/2, 0)
    .lineTo(L/2, H)
    .lineTo(L/2 - W_arm, H)
    .lineTo(L/2 - W_arm, H_base)
    .lineTo(-(L/2 - W_arm), H_base)
    .lineTo(-(L/2 - W_arm), H)
    .lineTo(-L/2, H)
    .lineTo(-L/2, 0)
    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Fillet main body outer bottom corners
def sel_body_outer_bot(edge):
    c = edge.center()
    return abs(c.z) < 0.1 and abs(c.x) > (L/2 - 0.1)

# Fillet main body inner corners
def sel_body_inner_cor(edge):
    c = edge.center()
    return abs(c.z - H_base) < 0.1 and abs(abs(c.x) - (L/2 - W_arm)) < 0.1

body = body.edges("%Y").filterBy(sel_body_outer_bot).fillet(r_out)
body = body.edges("%Y").filterBy(sel_body_inner_cor).fillet(r_in)

# 2. Front Pocket
p_out_x = L/2 - wt_side
p_in_x = L/2 - W_arm + wt_inner
p_bot_z = wt_bottom
p_in_bot_z = H_base - wt_inner
p_top_z = H - wt_top

body = (
    body.faces(">Y").workplane()
    .moveTo(0, p_bot_z)
    .lineTo(p_out_x, p_bot_z)
    .lineTo(p_out_x, p_top_z)
    .lineTo(p_in_x, p_top_z)
    .lineTo(p_in_x, p_in_bot_z)
    .lineTo(-p_in_x, p_in_bot_z)
    .lineTo(-p_in_x, p_top_z)
    .lineTo(-p_out_x, p_top_z)
    .lineTo(-p_out_x, p_bot_z)
    .close()
    .cutBlind(-pocket_depth)
)

# Fillet pocket edges
def sel_pocket_edges(edge):
    c = edge.center()
    return abs(c.y - (thickness - pocket_depth/2)) < 0.1

body = body.edges("%Y").filterBy(sel_pocket_edges).fillet(r_pocket)

# 3. Tabs
tabs_wp = cq.Workplane("XY", origin=(0, thickness, 0))
for x in tab_x:
    tabs_wp = tabs_wp.moveTo(x, tab_l/2).rect(tab_w, tab_l)

tabs = tabs_wp.extrude(tab_t)

# Fillet the front vertical edges of the tabs before union
def sel_tab_front_edges(edge):
    c = edge.center()
    return abs(c.y - (thickness + tab_l)) < 0.1

tabs = tabs.edges("|Z").filterBy(sel_tab_front_edges).fillet(tab_front_fillet)

# 4. Combine and apply connection fillets
result = body.union(tabs)

def sel_tab_root_fillets(edge):
    c = edge.center()
    return abs(c.z - tab_t) < 0.1 and abs(c.y - thickness) < 0.1

result = result.edges("%X").filterBy(sel_tab_root_fillets).fillet(tab_fillet)

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the wrench/tool geometry
thickness = 2.0
handle_width = 18.0
handle_length = 110.0
hole_size = 6.0
hole_spacing = 12.0
num_holes = 7

# 1. Create the base outer profile using a 2D sketch
s = cq.Sketch()

# Main handle body
s = s.push([(0, handle_length / 2)]).rect(handle_width, handle_length)

# Rounded bottom of the handle
s = s.push([(0, 0)]).circle(handle_width / 2, mode='a')

# Right arm
s = s.push([(22, 100)]).rect(40, 26, mode='a')

# Left arm (angled)
left_arm = cq.Sketch().push([(0, 0)]).rect(45, 26)
left_arm = left_arm.moved(
    cq.Location(cq.Vector(-24, 118, 0), cq.Vector(0, 0, 1), 40)
)
s = s.add(left_arm)

# Central filler to blend the arms and handle together
s = s.push([(0, 105)]).rect(30, 30, mode='a')

# Cutout notches on the top profile
s = s.push([(18, 120)]).circle(12, mode='s')
s = s.push([(-16, 132)]).circle(12, mode='s')

# 2. Extrude the profile into a 3D solid
result = cq.Workplane("XY").placeSketch(s).extrude(thickness)

# 3. Apply a fillet to all vertical edges to give it a smooth, stamped look
result = result.edges("|Z").fillet(2.5)

# 4. Create and cut the square holes along the handle
# Generating the center points for the square holes
hole_pts = [(0, 12 + i * hole_spacing) for i in range(num_holes)]

# Extrude the holes (slightly larger than thickness to ensure a clean cut)
holes = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .pushPoints(hole_pts)
    .rect(hole_size, hole_size)
    .extrude(thickness * 2)
    .translate((0, 0, -thickness / 2))
)

result = result.cut(holes)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
H = 100.0
W = 35.0
D = 18.0
left_leg_w = 12.0
back_web_d = 5.0
right_lip_w = 4.0
right_lip_d = 10.0
chamfer_val = 1.0

# Define the base profile points on the XY plane
pts = [
    (0, 0),
    (W, 0),
    (W, right_lip_d),
    (W - right_lip_w, right_lip_d),
    (W - right_lip_w, back_web_d),
    (left_leg_w, back_web_d),
    (left_leg_w, D),
    (0, D)
]

# Create the main extruded body
base = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(pts).close().extrude(H)

# Custom edge selector to apply chamfer to all edges EXCEPT the inner vertical concave edges
class ChamferEdges(cq.Selector):
    def __init__(self, y_val, x_val1, x_val2):
        self.y_val = y_val
        self.x_val1 = x_val1
        self.x_val2 = x_val2
        
    def filter(self, objectList):
        res = []
        for o in objectList:
            try:
                v0, v1 = o.startPoint(), o.endPoint()
                # Check if the edge is vertical (parallel to Z-axis)
                is_vertical = abs(v0.x - v1.x) < 1e-3 and abs(v0.y - v1.y) < 1e-3
                
                if is_vertical:
                    # Exclude the two specific inner concave vertical edges
                    if abs(v0.y - self.y_val) < 1e-3 and (abs(v0.x - self.x_val1) < 1e-3 or abs(v0.x - self.x_val2) < 1e-3):
                        continue
                res.append(o)
            except Exception:
                # In case of any geometric evaluation error, keep the edge
                res.append(o)
        return res

# Apply chamfer
result = base.edges(ChamferEdges(back_web_d, left_leg_w, W - right_lip_w)).chamfer(chamfer_val)

# Add counterbored hole on the left face
result = result.faces("<X").workplane().cboreHole(6.0, 11.0, 2.5)

pts = []
for i in range(n_points * 2):
    angle = i * math.pi / n_points + (math.pi / 2)
    r = r_out if i % 2 == 0 else r_in
    pts.append((r * math.cos(angle), r * math.sin(angle)))

# Create model
result = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")

    .close()
    .extrude(thickness)
    .faces(">Z")

)

# Optional: adding a chamfer to the central hole to match the visual depth/shading
try:

except:
    pass

import cadquery as cq
import math

# ==========================================
# Parameters
# ==========================================
L_flat = 50.0          # Length of the top flat section
L_slope = 80.0         # Horizontal length of the sloped section
W = 140.0              # Total width of the ramp
T = 8.0                # Thickness of the flat base section
D = 16.0               # Drop height of the sloped tip
pin_radius = 2.0       # Radius of the side pins
pin_stickout = 4.0     # How far the pins stick out on each side
rib_width = 2.5        # Width of the anti-slip ribs
rib_height = 1.5       # Height of the anti-slip ribs
rib_margin_x = 8.0     # Margin from the ends of the sloped face to the ribs
num_ribs = 11          # Number of anti-slip ribs
rib_spacing = 10.0     # Spacing between ribs

# ==========================================
# 1. Base Geometry
# ==========================================
# Define the side profile of the ramp
p1 = (-L_flat, 0)
p2 = (-L_flat, T)
p3 = (0, T)
p4 = (L_slope, -D)
p5 = (0, 0)

# Extrude the main body
base = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polyline([p1, p2, p3, p4, p5])
    .close()
    .extrude(W)
)

# ==========================================
# 2. Anti-Slip Ribs
# ==========================================
# Calculate properties of the sloped face for custom workplane
dx = L_slope
dz = -D - T
center_x = L_slope / 2.0
center_y = W / 2.0
center_z = (T - D) / 2.0

# Create a local plane aligned perfectly with the sloped face
# xDir points down the slope, normal points perpendicular up from the slope
slope_plane = cq.Plane(
    origin=(center_x, center_y, center_z),
    xDir=(dx, 0, dz),
    normal=(-dz, 0, dx)
)

L_sloped_face = math.sqrt(dx**2 + dz**2)
rib_length = L_sloped_face - 2 * rib_margin_x

# Generate points for the ribs centered across the width
start_y = - (num_ribs - 1) * rib_spacing / 2.0
pts = [(0, start_y + i * rib_spacing) for i in range(num_ribs)]

# Extrude the ribs normal to the sloped face
ribs = (
    cq.Workplane(slope_plane)
    .pushPoints(pts)
    .rect(rib_length, rib_width)
    .extrude(rib_height)
)

base = base.union(ribs)

# ==========================================
# 3. Side Pins (Hinge Pins)
# ==========================================
# Create the cylindrical pins at the corner vertex (0, 0)
pin = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(0, -pin_stickout, 0))
    .center(0, 0)
    .circle(pin_radius)
    .extrude(W + 2 * pin_stickout)
)

# Combine all features into the final result
result = base.union(pin)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
L = 100            # Overall length/width in X
W = 60             # Overall depth in Y
H = 30             # Overall height in Z
lip_w = 4          # Side recess width (top lip overhang)
lip_t = 5          # Top lip thickness
cut_w = 32         # Top U-cut width
cut_d = 16         # Top U-cut depth
R_bot = 12         # Bottom cutouts radius
cx_bot = 26        # Bottom cutouts center X positions
hill_y_start = 24  # Start position of the curved middle hill in Y
hill_R = 12        # Radius of the curved middle hill
chamfer_size = 10  # Chamfer size for the back corners

# Calculate main body width after side recesses
x_main = L / 2 - lip_w

# 1. Base solid profile (drawn on XZ plane, extruded along Y)
s = cq.Workplane("XZ").moveTo(-x_main, 0) \
    .lineTo(x_main, 0) \
    .lineTo(x_main, H - lip_t) \
    .lineTo(L / 2, H - lip_t) \
    .lineTo(L / 2, H) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2, H) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2, H - lip_t) \
    .lineTo(-x_main, H - lip_t) \
    .close()

result = s.extrude(W)

# 2. Bottom semicircular cutouts
bot_cuts = cq.Workplane("XZ") \
    .moveTo(-cx_bot, 0).circle(R_bot) \
    .moveTo(cx_bot, 0).circle(R_bot) \
    .extrude(W)

result = result.cut(bot_cuts)

# 3. Top U-cut along Y axis
u_cut = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=H - cut_d) \
    .center(0, W / 2).rect(cut_w, W).extrude(cut_d + 5)

result = result.cut(u_cut)

# 4. Middle curved hill feature resting in the U-cut
hill_len = W - hill_y_start
hill = cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=hill_y_start) \
    .moveTo(hill_R, H - cut_d) \
    .threePointArc((0, H - cut_d + hill_R), (-hill_R, H - cut_d)) \
    .lineTo(hill_R, H - cut_d) \
    .close() \
    .extrude(hill_len)

result = result.union(hill)

# 5. Chamfers on the back-left and back-right corners
corner_cut1 = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1) \
    .moveTo(-L / 2, W - chamfer_size) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2 + chamfer_size, W) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2 + chamfer_size, W + 5) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2 - 5, W + 5) \
    .lineTo(-L / 2 - 5, W - chamfer_size) \
    .close().extrude(H + 2)

corner_cut2 = cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1) \
    .moveTo(L / 2, W - chamfer_size) \
    .lineTo(L / 2 - chamfer_size, W) \
    .lineTo(L / 2 - chamfer_size, W + 5) \
    .lineTo(L / 2 + 5, W + 5) \
    .lineTo(L / 2 + 5, W - chamfer_size) \
    .close().extrude(H + 2)

result = result.cut(corner_cut1).cut(corner_cut2)

y_top_lip_b = H / 2 - lip_r_h
y_top_l_in = H / 2 - t_flange_l

    (x_right_out, y_top_out),       # Top-right outer corner
    (x_right_out, y_top_lip_b),     # Top-right lip bottom-right
    (x_right_in, y_top_lip_b),      # Top-right lip bottom-left
    (x_right_in, y_top_in),         # Top-right inner corner
    (x_web_r, y_top_in),            # Web right top inner corner
    (x_web_r, -y_top_in),           # Web right bottom inner corner
    (x_right_in, -y_top_in),        # Bottom-right inner corner
    (x_right_in, -y_top_lip_b),     # Bottom-right lip top-left
    (x_right_out, -y_top_lip_b),    # Bottom-right lip top-right
    (x_right_out, -y_top_out),      # Bottom-right outer corner
    (x_left_out, -y_top_out),       # Bottom-left outer corner
    (x_left_out, -y_top_l_lip_b),   # Bottom-left lip top-left
    (x_left_in, -y_top_l_lip_b),    # Bottom-left lip top-right

    # Fillets connecting web and right flanges
    .edges("|Z").edges(cq.selectors.BoxSelector((x_web_r-0.1, -H, -D*2), (x_web_r+0.1, H, D*2))).fillet(4.0)

    # Rounding the thin left sheet edges (inside)
    .edges("|Z").edges(cq.selectors.BoxSelector((x_left_in-0.1, -H, -D*2), (x_left_in+0.1, H, D*2))).fillet(1.9)
    # Rounding the thin left sheet edges (outside)
    .edges("|Z").edges(cq.selectors.BoxSelector((x_left_out-0.1, -H, -D*2), (x_left_out+0.1, H, D*2))).fillet(1.9)

# Rotate to match orientation in image (standing up)

import cadquery as cq

# Dimensions
L = 100
W = 60
H = 25
t = 5
flange_w = 10
cutout_L = 25

# 1. Create the outer box and fillet its vertical corners
outer_box = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=H / 2)
    .box(L, W, H)
    .edges("|Z")
    .fillet(3)
)

# 2. Create the lower part of the channel void
# This space is under the flanges. We make it slightly longer (L+2) 
# to ensure it cuts cleanly through the front and back ends.
void_bottom = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=t + (H - 2 * t) / 2)
    .box(L + 2, W - 2 * t, H - 2 * t)
    .edges("<Z and |X")
    .fillet(2)
)

# 3. Create the upper part of the channel void (the top opening and cutouts)
# The shape is a cross/I-shape sketched from the top view.
top_void_sketch = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=H - t)
    .moveTo(25, 15)
    .lineTo(25, 25)
    .lineTo(55, 25)
    .lineTo(55, -25)
    .lineTo(25, -25)
    .lineTo(25, -15)
    .lineTo(-25, -15)
    .lineTo(-25, -25)
    .lineTo(-55, -25)
    .lineTo(-55, 25)
    .lineTo(-25, 25)
    .lineTo(-25, 15)
    .close()
    .extrude(t + 5) # Extrude higher than the bounding box to ensure a clean cut
)

# Fillet the vertical edges of this top void to create the smooth sweeping transitions 
# where the flanges meet the side walls and the cutout corners.
top_void = top_void_sketch.edges("|Z").fillet(4)

# 4. Combine the voids into a single cutting tool
total_void = void_bottom.union(top_void)

# 5. Subtract the void from the outer box
result = outer_box.cut(total_void)

# 6. Apply a small fillet to the top edges to soften the profile as seen in the image
result = result.edges(">Z").fillet(1)

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for parametric design
base_radius = 25.0
base_thickness = 3.0

post_size = 6.0
post_fillet = 0.95

cross_width = 4.0
cross_height = 15.0

ring_out_radius = 18.0
ring_in_radius = 12.0
ring_height = 9.0

# 1. Build Base
base = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(base_radius).extrude(base_thickness)

# 2. Build Central Post
post = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .workplane(offset=base_thickness)
        .rect(post_size, post_size)
        .extrude(cross_height)
        .edges("|Z")
        .fillet(post_fillet))

# 3. Build Cross Arms
arm_dist = post_size / 2.0
arm_len = base_radius - arm_dist
arm_center = arm_dist + arm_len / 2.0

arms_x = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .workplane(offset=base_thickness)
          .pushPoints([(arm_center, 0), (-arm_center, 0)])
          .rect(arm_len, cross_width)
          .extrude(cross_height))

arms_y = (cq.Workplane("XY")
          .workplane(offset=base_thickness)
          .pushPoints([(0, arm_center), (0, -arm_center)])
          .rect(cross_width, arm_len)
          .extrude(cross_height))

# 4. Build Internal Ring Segments
ring = (cq.Workplane("XY")
        .workplane(offset=base_thickness)
        .circle(ring_out_radius)
        .circle(ring_in_radius)
        .extrude(ring_height))

# 5. Combine all components into the final solid
result = base.union(post).union(arms_x).union(arms_y).union(ring)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric Dimensions
L = 100.0
W = 75.0
H = 12.0
wall_t = 3.0
floor_t = 2.0
corner_r = 6.0

ear_r = 7.0
ear_h = 5.0
ear_hole_r = 2.0
ear_csk_r = 4.5
ear_spacing_x = 55.0

boss_r = 2.2
boss_hole_r = 1.0
boss_h = 4.0

groove_depth = 1.5
groove_width = 1.2

# 1. Main Base Body
base = cq.Workplane("XY").rect(L, W).extrude(H)
base = base.edges("|Z").fillet(corner_r)

# 2. Main Cavity
pocket = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=floor_t)
    .rect(L - 2*wall_t, W - 2*wall_t)
    .extrude(H)
    .edges("|Z").fillet(max(0.1, corner_r - wall_t))
)
body = base.cut(pocket)

# 3. Top Groove (Gasket Channel)
g_center_L = L - wall_t
g_center_W = W - wall_t
g_center_R = corner_r - wall_t/2.0

g_out_L = g_center_L + groove_width
g_out_W = g_center_W + groove_width
g_out_R = g_center_R + groove_width/2.0

g_in_L = g_center_L - groove_width
g_in_W = g_center_W - groove_width
g_in_R = g_center_R - groove_width/2.0

groove_sketch = (
    cq.Sketch()
    .rect(g_out_L, g_out_W).vertices().fillet(g_out_R)
    .rect(g_in_L, g_in_W, mode='s').vertices().fillet(max(0.1, g_in_R))
)

body = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane()
    .placeSketch(groove_sketch)
    .cutBlind(-groove_depth)
)

# 4. Mounting Ears
ear_pts = [
    (ear_spacing_x/2, W/2),
    (-ear_spacing_x/2, W/2),
    (ear_spacing_x/2, -W/2),
    (-ear_spacing_x/2, -W/2)
]

ears = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .pushPoints(ear_pts)
    .circle(ear_r)
    .extrude(ear_h)
)
body = body.union(ears)

# 5. Mounting Ear Countersunk Holes
body = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=ear_h - H)
    .pushPoints(ear_pts)
    .cskHole(diameter=ear_hole_r*2, cskDiameter=ear_csk_r*2, cskAngle=90)
)

# 6. Side Cutout Notch
cutout = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=floor_t)
    .moveTo(L/2, 0)
    .rect(wall_t * 3, 16)
    .extrude(H)
)
body = body.cut(cutout)

# 7. Internal Cylindrical Bosses
bx = L/2 - wall_t - boss_r - 2.5
by = W/2 - wall_t - boss_r - 2.5
boss_pts = [(bx, by), (-bx, by), (bx, -by), (-bx, -by)]

body = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=floor_t - H)
    .pushPoints(boss_pts)
    .circle(boss_r).extrude(boss_h)
)
body = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=floor_t + boss_h - H)
    .pushPoints(boss_pts)
    .circle(boss_hole_r).cutBlind(-boss_h)
)

# 8. Internal Square Bosses
sq_pts = [
    (-L/2 + wall_t + 14, W/2 - wall_t - 3),
    (-L/2 + wall_t + 3, W/2 - wall_t - 14)
]
body = (
    body.faces(">Z").workplane(offset=floor_t - H)
    .pushPoints(sq_pts)
    .rect(4.5, 4.5).extrude(boss_h)
)

result = body

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
w = 20          # Width of the part
r_out = 15      # Outer radius of the main arch
r_in = 10       # Inner radius of the main arch
tab_h = 5       # Height of the right base tab
block_h = 12    # Height of the left slot block

# 1. Main outer arch
# Cylinder axis is automatically Z when created on the 'front' workplane
outer_arch = cq.Workplane("front").cylinder(height=w, radius=r_out)

# 2. Right base tab
# Extends from X=0 to X=20, height 5. Center is at X=10, Y=2.5.
right_tab = cq.Workplane("front").moveTo(10, tab_h/2).box(20, tab_h, w)

# Rounded end for the right tab
# Placed on XZ plane offset by tab_h/2 (Y=2.5). Center at X=20, Z=0.
tab_end = (cq.Workplane("XZ")
           .workplane(offset=tab_h/2)
           .moveTo(20, 0)
           .cylinder(height=tab_h, radius=w/2))

# 3. Left slot block
# Extends from X=-28 to X=0 to perfectly merge with the arch. Height 12.
left_block = cq.Workplane("front").moveTo(-14, block_h/2).box(28, block_h, w)

# Combine the base solid bodies
result = outer_arch.union(right_tab).union(tab_end).union(left_block)

# Cut the bottom half to create the flat base
bottom_cut = cq.Workplane("front").moveTo(0, -25).box(100, 50, w*2)
result = result.cut(bottom_cut)

# Cut the inner arch
inner_arch = cq.Workplane("front").cylinder(height=w*2, radius=r_in)
result = result.cut(inner_arch)

# 4. U-Slot cut in the left block
# Centered at X=-22, width 8, cuts down to Y=2
slot = (cq.Workplane("front")
        .moveTo(-22, 20)
        .lineTo(-18, 20)
        .lineTo(-18, 6)
        .threePointArc((-22, 2), (-26, 6))
        .lineTo(-26, 20)
        .close()
        .extrude(w*2, both=True))
result = result.cut(slot)

# 5. Small locating hole on top of the arch
result = (result.copyWorkplane(cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=r_out))
          .moveTo(0, 0)
          .hole(diameter=2, depth=10))

# 6. Countersunk mounting hole in the right tab
result = (result.copyWorkplane(cq.Workplane("XZ").workplane(offset=tab_h))
          .moveTo(22, 0)
          .cskHole(diameter=5, cskDiameter=9, cskAngle=90, depth=10))

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the geometry
block_size = 20.0
block_length = 30.0

shaft_diam = 16.0
shaft_length = 65.0

pin_diam = 8.0
pin_length = 12.0

hole_diam = 8.0
hole_depth = 12.0

flat_offset = 5.0
flat_gap = 6.0

# 1. Base Geometry (Block, Shaft, End Pin)
result = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .rect(block_size, block_size)
    .extrude(block_length)
)

# Add main shaft
result = (
    result.faces(">X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(shaft_diam / 2.0)
    .extrude(shaft_length)
)

# Add end pin
result = (
    result.faces(">X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(pin_diam / 2.0)
    .extrude(pin_length)
)

# Add hole in the back of the block
result = (
    result.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(hole_diam / 2.0)
    .cutBlind(-hole_depth)
)

# 2. Side Flat Cut on the Shaft
cut_len = shaft_length - flat_gap + 5.0  # Extend slightly past the shaft to avoid thin walls
cut_center_x = block_length + flat_gap + cut_len / 2.0
cut_y_size = 20.0
cut_center_y = flat_offset + cut_y_size / 2.0

cut_box = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .center(cut_center_x, cut_center_y)
    .box(cut_len, cut_y_size, shaft_diam + 5.0)
)
result = result.cut(cut_box)

# 3. Diagonal Grooves (Serrations) on the Flat
groove_width = 1.5
groove_depth = 2.0
groove_angle = 20.0

# Create a single groove cutting tool
groove_tool = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(groove_width, groove_depth * 2.0, shaft_diam + 5.0)
    .rotate((0, 0, 0), (0, 1, 0), groove_angle)
)

# Calculate pattern
groove_pitch = 4.5
num_grooves = int((shaft_length - flat_gap - 4.0) // groove_pitch)
start_x = block_length + flat_gap + 3.0

# Apply cuts iteratively
for i in range(num_grooves):
    gx = start_x + i * groove_pitch
    g = groove_tool.translate((gx, flat_offset, 0))
    result = result.cut(g)

# Parameters
width = 32.0          # Total width of the clevis
height = 30.0         # Height of the clevis
length = 35.0         # Length from the hole center to the back face
gap = 16.0            # Width of the inner U-cutout
radius = 15.0         # Radius of the rounded prongs
cutout_depth = 15.0   # Depth of cutout from the hole center towards the back
hole_radius = 6.0     # Radius of the through hole
shaft_radius = 8.0    # Radius of the back shaft
shaft_length = 25.0   # Length of the back shaft
chamfer_size = 1.0    # Chamfer size for the holes

# 1. Create the main block and front rounded ends
base = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, height, centered=(False, True, True))
    .translate((-length, 0, 0))
    .union(
        cq.Workplane("XZ")
        .circle(radius)
        .extrude(width / 2.0, both=True)
    )
)

# 2. Create the U-shape cutout to form the prongs
cutout = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(radius + cutout_depth + 5.0, gap, height + 5.0, centered=(False, True, True))
    .translate((-cutout_depth, 0, 0))
)
result = base.cut(cutout)

# 3. Add the through hole
hole_cut = cq.Workplane("XZ").circle(hole_radius).extrude(width / 2.0 + 5.0, both=True)
result = result.cut(hole_cut)

# 4. Chamfer the outer circular edges of the holes

# 5. Add the cylindrical shaft at the back
result = (
    result.faces("<X")
    .workplane()
    .circle(shaft_radius)
    .extrude(shaft_length)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
thickness = 5.0
r_out = 15.0
r_in = 10.0
dist = 24.0
fillet_radius = 2.0
edge_fillet = 0.5

# Create the base solid by uniting 5 overlapping cylinders
base = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .pushPoints([(0, 0), (dist, 0), (-dist, 0), (0, dist), (0, -dist)])
    .circle(r_out)
    .extrude(thickness)
)

# Custom edge selector to find the concave intersection lines
# The intersection edges will lie exactly on the perimeter of the central cylinder (radius = r_out)
def is_intersection(e):
    c = e.Center()
    r = (c.x**2 + c.y**2)**0.5
    return (r_out - 1.0) < r < (r_out + 1.0)

# Apply fillet to the internal intersections between the rings
result = base.edges("|Z and %Line").filterBy(is_intersection).fillet(fillet_radius)

# Cut the inner holes through the structure
result = (
    result.faces(">Z").workplane()
    .pushPoints([(0, 0), (dist, 0), (-dist, 0), (0, dist), (0, -dist)])
    .circle(r_in)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# Apply a small global fillet to smooth out all sharp edges, giving a realistic finished look
result = result.edges().fillet(edge_fillet)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
box_size = 100.0

hole_fillet = 8.0

result = result.edges().fillet(box_fillet)

# Create cutting cylinders for each axis (X, Y, Z)
# The cylinders are made longer than the box to ensure clean cuts
cyl_z = cq.Workplane("XY").cylinder(box_size * 2, hole_radius)
cyl_y = cq.Workplane("XZ").cylinder(box_size * 2, hole_radius)
cyl_x = cq.Workplane("YZ").cylinder(box_size * 2, hole_radius)

# Subtract the cylinders to create the through holes
result = result.cut(cyl_z).cut(cyl_y).cut(cyl_x)

# Apply a fillet to all the circular hole entrances
result = result.edges("%Circle").fillet(hole_fillet)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
shaft_radius = 1.5
shaft_length = 120.0

blade_half_width = 6.0
blade_half_thick = 1.0
blade_base_len = 15.0
blade_tip_len = 30.0

overlap = 2.0 

# Main cylindrical shaft
shaft = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(shaft_radius).extrude(shaft_length)

# Tapered extension of the shaft to form the central ridge on the spearhead
ridge = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=shaft_length)
    .circle(shaft_radius)
    .workplane(offset=blade_base_len)
    .circle(0.1)
    .loft()
)

# Spearhead blade profile coordinates
pts_base = [
    (0, shaft_radius), 
    (shaft_radius, 0), 
    (0, -shaft_radius), 
    (-shaft_radius, 0), 
    (0, shaft_radius)
]

pts_mid = [
    (0, blade_half_thick), 
    (blade_half_width, 0), 
    (0, -blade_half_thick), 
    (-blade_half_width, 0), 
    (0, blade_half_thick)
]

pts_tip = [
    (0, 0.01), 
    (0.01, 0), 
    (0, -0.01), 
    (-0.01, 0), 
    (0, 0.01)
]

# Create the spearhead blade using lofting
spearhead = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=shaft_length - overlap)
    .polyline(pts_base)
    .workplane(offset=blade_base_len + overlap)
    .polyline(pts_mid)
    .workplane(offset=blade_tip_len)
    .polyline(pts_tip)
    .loft()
)

# Combine all components into the final solid geometry
result = shaft.union(ridge).union(spearhead)

    (L1 + L2, W2 / 2.0),
    (L1 + L2, -W2 / 2.0),
    (L1, -W2 / 2.0),
    (L1, -W1 / 2.0),
    (0, -W1 / 2.0)
]

# Create the base plate and fillet outer edges
base = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(pts).close().extrude(t_plate)
base = base.edges("|Z").fillet(4.0)

# Cutout parameters
c1_l = 90.0
c1_w = 40.0
c1_x = 10.0 + c1_l / 2.0

c2_l = 25.0
c2_w = 40.0
c2_x = L1 + 10.0 + c2_l / 2.0

# Create cutouts with filleted inner corners
cutouts = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1.0)
    .center(c1_x, 0).rect(c1_l, c1_w)
    .center(c2_x - c1_x, 0).rect(c2_l, c2_w)
    .extrude(t_plate + 2.0)
    .edges("|Z").fillet(3.0)
)
result = base.cut(cutouts)

# Post (boss) parameters
post_ro = 3.5
post_ri = 1.8
post_h = 5.0

post_xs = [16.0, 94.0]
post_ys = [24.0, -24.0]
posts_centers = [(x, y) for x in post_xs for y in post_ys]

# Add posts
posts = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=t_plate)
    .pushPoints(posts_centers)
    .circle(post_ro)
    .extrude(post_h)
)
result = result.union(posts)

# Cut through holes for the posts
post_holes = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1.0)
    .pushPoints(posts_centers)
    .circle(post_ri)
    .extrude(t_plate + post_h + 2.0)
)
result = result.cut(post_holes)

# Plain holes on the right section
hole_r = 2.0
hole_xs = [L1 + 8.0, L1 + L2 - 8.0]
hole_ys = [32.0, -32.0]
hole_centers = [(x, y) for x in hole_xs for y in hole_ys]

plain_holes = (
    cq.Workplane("XY").workplane(offset=-1.0)
    .pushPoints(hole_centers)
    .circle(hole_r)
    .extrude(t_plate + 2.0)
)
result = result.cut(plain_holes)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters
D = 50.0          # Circumcircle diameter of a single hexagon
hole_D = 25.0     # Diameter of the holes
thickness = 8.0   # Thickness of the plate
chamfer_val = 1.5 # Chamfer distance

R = D / 2.0
dist = R * math.sqrt(3)

# Center points for the four hexagons (central + 3 surrounding)
angles = [30, 150, 270]
pts = [(0, 0)] + [
    (dist * math.cos(math.radians(a)), dist * math.sin(math.radians(a))) 
    for a in angles
]

# Create the base solid by unioning 4 hexagonal prisms
result = cq.Workplane("XY").polygon(6, D).extrude(thickness)

for p in pts[1:]:
    lobe = cq.Workplane("XY").center(*p).polygon(6, D).extrude(thickness)
    result = result.union(lobe)

# Cut the central and lobe holes
result = (
    result.faces(">Z").workplane()
    .pushPoints(pts)
    .circle(hole_D / 2.0)
    .cutThruAll()
)

# Chamfer the outer top and bottom edges 
# The outer boundary consists of line segments, while holes are circles
result = result.faces(">Z or <Z").edges("%LINE").chamfer(chamfer_val)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions
thickness = 8.0
center_hub_r = 18.0
center_hole_r = 11.0
arm_len = 38.0
lobe_hub_r = 16.0
lobe_hole_r = 11.0

# 1. Base central hub
result = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(center_hub_r).extrude(thickness)

# 2. Add arms, lobes, and swept blades
for i in range(3):
    angle = i * 120

    # Lobe
    lobe = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, angle))
        .center(arm_len, 0)
        .circle(lobe_hub_r)
        .extrude(thickness)
    )
    result = result.union(lobe)

    # Webbing connection
    web = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, angle))
        .center(arm_len / 2, 0)
        .box(arm_len, lobe_hub_r * 1.5, thickness)
    )
    result = result.union(web)

    # Swept curved blade extending from lobe
    blade = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, angle))
        .center(arm_len, 0)
        .moveTo(0, lobe_hub_r)
        .threePointArc((18, 5), (28, -20))  # Outer curve to sharp tip
        .threePointArc((19, -15), (12, -10)) # Inner curve back to lobe
        .lineTo(0, 0)                        # Anchor inside lobe for solid union
        .close()
        .extrude(thickness)
    )
    result = result.union(blade)

# 3. Apply large fillet to the webbing waist between center and lobes
def is_waist_edge(e):
    pt = e.BoundingBox().center
    r = math.hypot(pt.x, pt.y)
    # Target vertical edges formed by union of web, center hub, and lobes
    return 15 < r < arm_len - 2

result = result.edges("|Z").filterBy(is_waist_edge).fillet(7.0)

# 4. Cut the through-holes
hole_c = cq.Workplane("XY").circle(center_hole_r).extrude(thickness)
result = result.cut(hole_c)

for i in range(3):
    angle = i * 120
    hole_l = (
        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .transformed(rotate=cq.Vector(0, 0, angle))
        .center(arm_len, 0)
        .circle(lobe_hole_r)
        .extrude(thickness)
    )
    result = result.cut(hole_l)

# 5. Chamfer the internal hole edges for a smooth finish
def is_hole_edge(e):
    if e.geomType() == 'CIRCLE':
        try:
            return abs(e.radius() - center_hole_r) < 0.1 or abs(e.radius() - lobe_hole_r) < 0.1
        except:
            pass
    return False

result = result.edges().filterBy(is_hole_edge).chamfer(1.2)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
d_flange = 40.0
r_flange = d_flange / 2.0
w_flange1 = 8.0
w_flange2 = 10.0

r_valley = 8.0
r_peak1 = 12.0

r_mid1 = 16.0
w_mid1 = 8.0
r_gap = 10.0
w_gap = 8.0
r_mid2 = 18.0
w_mid2 = 8.0

groove1_pitch = 5.0
groove1_count = 4

groove2_pitch = 6.0
groove2_count = 4

r_pin = 1.0
l_pin = 8.0

pts = []
pts.append((0, 0))

# Left Flange
pts.append((0, r_flange))
x = w_flange1
pts.append((x, r_flange))
pts.append((x, r_valley))

# Grooves 1
for i in range(groove1_count):
    x += groove1_pitch / 2.0
    pts.append((x, r_peak1))
    x += groove1_pitch / 2.0
    pts.append((x, r_valley))

# Mid flange 1
pts.append((x, r_mid1))
x += w_mid1
pts.append((x, r_mid1))

# Gap
pts.append((x, r_gap))
x += w_gap
pts.append((x, r_gap))

# Mid flange 2
pts.append((x, r_mid2))
x += w_mid2
pts.append((x, r_mid2))

# Grooves 2
pts.append((x, r_valley))
for i in range(groove2_count):
    x += groove2_pitch / 2.0
    peak_radius = 10.0 + i * ((r_flange - 1.0 - 10.0) / (groove2_count - 1))
    pts.append((x, peak_radius))
    x += groove2_pitch / 2.0
    pts.append((x, r_valley))

# Right Flange
pts.append((x, r_flange))
x += w_flange2
pts.append((x, r_flange))

# Pin
pts.append((x, r_pin))
x += l_pin
pts.append((x, r_pin))
pts.append((x, 0))

# Close profile
pts.append((0, 0))

# Generate revolved solid
result = cq.Workplane("XY").polyline(pts).revolve(360, (0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0))

# Parametric dimensions
L = 40.0         # Overall length and height

    (0, 0),

    (W, W),
    (W, L),
    (0, L)
]

    result.faces(">Z")
    .workplane(origin=(W/2.0, D/2.0, L))
    .cboreHole(hole_dia, cbore_dia, cbore_depth)
)

    result.faces(">X")
    .workplane(origin=(L, D/2.0, W/2.0))
    .cboreHole(hole_dia, cbore_dia, cbore_depth)
)

# Add Front corner hole (>Y face)
result = (
    result.faces(">Y")
    .workplane(origin=(W/2.0, D, W/2.0))
    .cboreHole(hole_dia, cbore_dia, cbore_depth)
)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions
L = 100.0        # Overall length of each arm
t = 15.0         # Thickness of the square struts
c = 3.5          # Chamfer distance for the ends
h_d = 7.0        # Diameter of the fixing holes
hole_depth = 30.0 # Depth of the fixing holes

# Calculate the inner vertex position to maintain uniform strut thickness for the diagonals
inner_v = L - t * math.sqrt(2)

# Define outer and inner 2D boundaries for a single plate frame
pts_outer = [
    (0, 0),
    (L, 0),
    (L, t),
    (t, L),
    (0, L)
]

pts_inner = [
    (t, t),
    (inner_v, t),
    (t, inner_v)
]

# Generate the three orthogonal plates with triangular cutouts
xz_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ")
    .polyline(pts_outer).close()
    .polyline(pts_inner).close()
    .extrude(t)
)

xy_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polyline(pts_outer).close()
    .polyline(pts_inner).close()
    .extrude(t)
)

yz_plate = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    .polyline(pts_outer).close()
    .polyline(pts_inner).close()
    .extrude(t)
)

# Combine the plates to form the 3D tetrahedral truss structure
result = xz_plate.union(xy_plate).union(yz_plate)

# Chamfer the outer edges of the three end faces to create an octagonal profile
result = (
    result
    .edges(">X").chamfer(c)
    .edges(">Y").chamfer(c)
    .edges(">Z").chamfer(c)
)

# Drill mounting holes at the three extremities
result = (
    result
    .faces(">X").workplane().hole(h_d, depth=hole_depth)
    .faces(">Y").workplane().hole(h_d, depth=hole_depth)
    .faces(">Z").workplane().hole(h_d, depth=hole_depth)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
width = 75
length = 160
height = 8
thickness = 2
corner_radius = 8
inner_fillet = 2

# Create the outer solid body
outer = cq.Workplane("XY").box(width, length, height, centered=(True, True, False))
outer = outer.edges("|Z").fillet(corner_radius)
outer = outer.edges("<Z").fillet(1)  # Small fillet on the bottom outer edge

# Create the inner void to hollow out the tray
inner_width = width - 2 * thickness
inner_length = length - 2 * thickness
inner_height = height

inner = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, 0, thickness))
    .box(inner_width, inner_length, inner_height, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges("|Z").fillet(corner_radius - thickness)
    .edges("<Z").fillet(inner_fillet)  # Inner bottom edge fillet
)

# Subtract inner from outer to get the base tray
tray = outer - inner

# Cut the bottom wall opening (-Y side)
cut_bottom_width = width - 2 * corner_radius
cut_bottom = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(0, -length / 2, thickness))
    .box(cut_bottom_width, thickness * 4, height, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges("|Z").fillet(1.5)  # Round the edges of the cut for a smooth drop-off
)
tray = tray - cut_bottom

# Cut the left side wall opening (-X side)
cut_left_len = 70
cut_left = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .transformed(offset=(-width / 2, -15, thickness))
    .box(thickness * 4, cut_left_len, height, centered=(True, True, False))
    .edges("|Z").fillet(1.5)
)
tray = tray - cut_left

# Add the small slot on the top right wall (+Y side)
slot_length_center_to_center = 10
slot_diameter = 2
slot_center_x = 18
slot_center_z = 5

slot_cutter = (
    cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(0, length / 2 - thickness * 2, 0))
    .center(slot_center_x, slot_center_z)
    .slot2D(slot_length_center_to_center, slot_diameter, 0)
    .extrude(thickness * 4)
)
tray = tray - slot_cutter

# Fillet the top edges of the tray for a smooth lip finish
result = tray.edges(cq.selectors.ZMaxSelector()).fillet(0.5)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions
outer_radius = 50.0
inner_radius = 19.1  # Golden ratio proportion for a perfect 5-pointed star
thickness = 10.0
hole_radius = 8.0
fillet_radius = 2.0
num_points = 5

# Generate points for the star profile
pts = []
for i in range(num_points * 2):
    angle = math.radians(i * 360.0 / (num_points * 2) + 90)
    rad = outer_radius if i % 2 == 0 else inner_radius
    x = rad * math.cos(angle)
    y = rad * math.sin(angle)
    pts.append((x, y))

    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .polyline(pts)
    .close()
    .circle(hole_radius)
    .extrude(thickness)
    .edges()
    .fillet(fillet_radius)
)

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters for the parametric model
thickness = 20
rect_thickness = 12
center_r = 14
lobe_r = 10
lobe_dist = 16
rect_w = 18
rect_x_end = -30
fillet_radius = 5
hole_dia = 12
csk_dia = 22
csk_angle = 90

# Create the main central cylinder
main = cq.Workplane("XY").cylinder(thickness, center_r, centered=(True, True, False))

# Create the three cylindrical lobes
lobe1 = cq.Workplane("XY").center(lobe_dist, 0).cylinder(thickness, lobe_r, centered=(True, True, False))
lobe2 = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, lobe_dist).cylinder(thickness, lobe_r, centered=(True, True, False))
lobe3 = cq.Workplane("XY").center(0, -lobe_dist).cylinder(thickness, lobe_r, centered=(True, True, False))

# Combine main body and lobes
base_body = main.union(lobe1).union(lobe2).union(lobe3)

# Create the rectangular extension
rect_l = abs(rect_x_end)
rect = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=thickness - rect_thickness)
    .center(rect_x_end / 2, 0)
    .box(rect_l, rect_w, rect_thickness, centered=(True, True, False))
)

# Combine with rectangular extension
solid_body = base_body.union(rect)

# Apply a fillet to the bottom outer edge of the rectangular extension
fillet_point = (rect_x_end, 0, thickness - rect_thickness)
solid_body = solid_body.edges(cq.selectors.NearestToPointSelector(fillet_point)).fillet(fillet_radius)

# Add the central countersunk hole on the top face
result = solid_body.faces(">Z").workplane().cskHole(
    diameter=hole_dia, 
    cskDiameter=csk_dia, 
    cskAngle=csk_angle
)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parametric dimensions for the spinner
thickness = 7.0
arm_len = 30.0
lobe_r = 15.0
center_hole_r = 10.5
outer_hole_r = 10.5
web_fillet = 10.0
edge_fillet = 2.0

# Custom selector to target only the inner vertical intersection edges for the concave web
class InnerEdges(cq.Selector):
    def filter(self, objectList):
        # The inner corners are at a radius of ~17mm. The outer lobe edges are at R=45mm.
        return [o for o in objectList if (o.Center().x**2 + o.Center().y**2)**0.5 < 25]

def make_lobe(angle):
    # Create the straight arm segment slightly over-extending past origin
    arm = (cq.Workplane("XY")
           .box(arm_len + 10, 2 * lobe_r, thickness, centered=(False, True, True))
           .translate((-10, 0, 0)))
    
    # Create the outer rounded lobe
    cyl = (cq.Workplane("XY")
           .center(arm_len, 0)
           .cylinder(thickness, lobe_r))
    
    # Combine and rotate to the correct orientation
    return arm.union(cyl).rotate((0,0,0), (0,0,1), angle)

# 1. Build Base Shape (Three lobes at 120-degree intervals)
spinner = make_lobe(90)
for a in [210, 330]:
    spinner = spinner.union(make_lobe(a))

# 2. Fillet the 3 inner sharp vertical corners to create the smooth concave web
spinner = spinner.edges("%Z").edges(InnerEdges()).fillet(web_fillet)

# 3. Cut Central Hole
spinner = spinner.faces(">Z").workplane().hole(2 * center_hole_r)

# 4. Cut Outer Lobe Holes
for angle in [90, 210, 330]:
    rad = math.radians(angle)
    cx = arm_len * math.cos(rad)
    cy = arm_len * math.sin(rad)
    spinner = spinner.faces(">Z").workplane().center(cx, cy).hole(2 * outer_hole_r)

# 5. Fillet all top and bottom horizontal edges (both outer rims and inner hole rims)
result = spinner.edges("not %Z").fillet(edge_fillet)

import cadquery as cq
import math

# Parameters for the fidget spinner
thickness = 7.0
center_hole_d = 22.0
center_od = 30.0
outer_hole_d = 22.0
outer_od = 30.0
arm_length = 40.0
web_width = 16.0
fillet_radius = 5.0

# Start building the 2D profile
sketch = cq.Sketch().circle(center_od / 2)

# Add arms (connecting webs and outer rings)
for a in [0, 120, 240]:
    rad = math.radians(a)
    
    # Connecting web
    cx = (arm_length / 2) * math.cos(rad)
    cy = (arm_length / 2) * math.sin(rad)
    sketch = sketch.reset().push([(cx, cy)]).rect(arm_length, web_width, angle=a)
    
    # Outer ring
    ox = arm_length * math.cos(rad)
    oy = arm_length * math.sin(rad)
    sketch = sketch.reset().push([(ox, oy)]).circle(outer_od / 2)

# Fillet the sharp interior intersections of the united shape
sketch = sketch.reset().vertices().fillet(fillet_radius)

# Subtract center hole
sketch = sketch.reset().circle(center_hole_d / 2, mode='s')

# Subtract outer bearing holes
for a in [0, 120, 240]:
    rad = math.radians(a)
    ox = arm_length * math.cos(rad)
    oy = arm_length * math.sin(rad)
    sketch = sketch.reset().push([(ox, oy)]).circle(outer_hole_d / 2, mode='s')

# Extrude the completed sketch into a 3D solid
result = cq.Workplane("XY").placeSketch(sketch).extrude(thickness)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions for the model
length = 100.0
width = 70.0
thickness = 12.0
outer_corner_chamfer = 14.0
wall_thickness = 10.0
pocket_depth = 6.0
top_chamfer = 3.0

# Derived dimensions for the inner pocket
pocket_length = length - 2 * wall_thickness
pocket_width = width - 2 * wall_thickness
# Maintain uniform wall thickness at the corners
pocket_corner_chamfer = outer_corner_chamfer - wall_thickness 

# 1. Create the base rectangular block and chamfer the outer vertical corners
base = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, thickness)
    .edges("|Z")
    .chamfer(outer_corner_chamfer)
)

# 2. Cut the inner pocket with matching chamfered corners
result = (
    base.faces(">Z")
    .workplane()
    .sketch()
    .rect(pocket_length, pocket_width)
    .vertices()
    .chamfer(pocket_corner_chamfer)
    .finalize()
    .extrude(-pocket_depth, combine="cut")
)

# 3. Chamfer all edges on the top flat face (both inner and outer perimeters)
result = result.faces(">Z").edges().chamfer(top_chamfer)

import cadquery as cq

# Parametric dimensions
W = 60.0
D = 40.0
H = 50.0
t = 2.0
flange_start_X = 35.0

# Create main shell components
base = cq.Workplane("XY").box(W, D, t, centered=(False, False, False))
left_wall = cq.Workplane("XY").box(t, D, H, centered=(False, False, False))
right_wall = cq.Workplane("XY").moveTo(W-t, 0).box(t, D, H, centered=(False, False, False))
back_wall = cq.Workplane("XY").moveTo(0, D-t).box(W, t, H, centered=(False, False, False))
flange = cq.Workplane("XY").moveTo(flange_start_X, 0).box(W - flange_start_X, t, H, centered=(False, False, False))

# Combine main body
result = base.union(left_wall).union(right_wall).union(back_wall).union(flange)

# Hinge ears properties
ear_z1 = 15.0
ear_z2 = 35.0
ear_r = 4.0

# Add hinge ears
ear1 = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(flange_start_X, 0, ear_z1)).circle(ear_r).extrude(t)
ear2 = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(flange_start_X, 0, ear_z2)).circle(ear_r).extrude(t)
result = result.union(ear1).union(ear2)

# Cut hinge pin holes
hole_r = 1.5
hole1 = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(flange_start_X, -1, ear_z1)).circle(hole_r).extrude(t+2)
hole2 = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(flange_start_X, -1, ear_z2)).circle(hole_r).extrude(t+2)
result = result.cut(hole1).cut(hole2)

# Flange cutout geometry (creates the complex C-shaped opening with the tab)
# Main vertical slot aligned with ears
slot = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(41, -1, 25)).rect(6, 28).extrude(t+2)
# Top large opening
top_hole = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(50, -1, 34.5)).rect(12, 9).extrude(t+2)
# Bottom large opening
bot_hole = cq.Workplane("XZ", origin=(50, -1, 15.5)).rect(12, 9).extrude(t+2)

result = result.cut(slot).cut(top_hole).cut(bot_hole)

# Left wall access hole
left_hole = cq.Workplane("YZ", origin=(-1, 20, 25)).circle(7.0).extrude(t+2)
result = result.cut(left_hole)

import cadquery as cq

# Define parametric dimensions
length = 120.0
width = 90.0
height = 12.0
wall_thickness = 4.0
floor_thickness = 3.0
outer_corner_radius = 12.0
inner_bottom_fillet = 3.0
top_rim_fillet = 1.5

# Calculate derived dimensions for the inner hollow section (pocket)
inner_length = length - 2.0 * wall_thickness
inner_width = width - 2.0 * wall_thickness
inner_height = height - floor_thickness + 5.0  # Extend slightly above to ensure clean cut
inner_corner_radius = outer_corner_radius - wall_thickness
z_offset = (-height / 2.0) + floor_thickness + (inner_height / 2.0)

# Create the main solid block and fillet its vertical corners
outer = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .box(length, width, height)
    .edges("|Z")
    .fillet(outer_corner_radius)
)

# Create the inner volume to be subtracted, applying fillets to corners and bottom
inner = (
    cq.Workplane("XY")
    .workplane(offset=z_offset)
    .box(inner_length, inner_width, inner_height)
    .edges("|Z")
    .fillet(inner_corner_radius)
    .edges("<Z")
    .fillet(inner_bottom_fillet)
)

# Subtract the inner volume from the outer volume to create the tray shape
result = outer.cut(inner)

# Apply fillets to the inner and outer edges of the top rim
result = result.edges(">Z").fillet(top_rim_fillet)

import cadquery as cq

# Parameters
length = 120.0
width = 25.0
height = 50.0
fillet_radius = 8.0

slot_depth = 10.0
slot_height = 6.0
slot_offset_top = 6.0

# Create the profile on the YZ plane and extrude along X
result = (
    cq.Workplane("YZ")
    # Start at top-right corner
    .moveTo(width / 2, height / 2)
    # Draw to top-left
    .lineTo(-width / 2, height / 2)
    # Draw to bottom-left
    .lineTo(-width / 2, -height / 2)
    # Draw to bottom-right
    .lineTo(width / 2, -height / 2)
    # Draw up to bottom of the slot
    .lineTo(width / 2, height / 2 - slot_offset_top - slot_height)
    # Draw inward (slot depth)
    .lineTo(width / 2 - slot_depth, height / 2 - slot_offset_top - slot_height)
    # Draw upward (slot height)
    .lineTo(width / 2 - slot_depth, height / 2 - slot_offset_top)
    # Draw outward back to the right edge
    .lineTo(width / 2, height / 2 - slot_offset_top)
    # Close the profile back to top-right
    .close()
    # Extrude to create the 3D block
    .extrude(length)
    # Select the front face (min Y), then filter for edges parallel to the extrusion axis (X), and fillet them
    .edges("<Y")
    .edges("%X")
    .fillet(fillet_radius)
)

# Parameters

    .circle(inner_radius)

)

    cq.Workplane("XY")

        cq.Workplane("XY")
        .rect(rib_width, inner_radius * 2)

try:
    result = result.edges("|Z and (not >X and not <X and not >Y and not <Y)").fillet(2.0)
except:
    pass # Fallback in case edge selection fails

# Parameters
length = 100.0
width = 35.0

# Calculate compartment length
